evidence_quotes.quote: Hundreds of games have already been made unavailable despite having been purchased
The servers running the game were shut down, leaving the consumers without anything from their purchase.
It is unfair to a consumer to have a product removed completely without being informed of when exactly that product would be removed at the time of the purchase.

paragraphs.text: Hello, This is a great act moving in the correct direction. I am voicing my opinion and request in wanting to include video games in this act. Hundreds of games have already been made unavailable despite having been purchased, a recent one that springs to mind is a game called "The crew". The servers running the game were shut down, leaving the consumers without anything from their purchase. I believe situations like this should also fall under this act. This is just one single example, but many games fall under the same category and are at risk of having their access removed completely. It is unfair to a consumer to have a product removed completely without being informed of when exactly that product would be removed at the time of the purchase. There is already a citizens initiative going for this, or something similar, with well over 1.000.000 signatures, but I believe this act could encompass that same issue at the same time. Thank you for reading and taking feedback!
